505: 				Leaf:        parsedCert,
506: 			},
507: 		},

510: 		MinVersion: tls.VersionTLS13,
511: 	}

515: 	transport.TLSClientConfig = tlsConf

520: 	c.listener, err = net.ListenTCP("tcp", &net.TCPAddr{
521: 		IP:   listenAddr,
522: 		Port: c.flagListenPort,
523: 	})

527: 	}

536: 	}

539: 	defer func() {
540: 		c.listenerCloseOnce.Do(listenerCloseFunc)
541: 	}()

544: 
545: 	if c.flagExec == "" {
546: 		sessInfo := SessionInfo{
547: 			Protocol:        "tcp",
548: 			Address:         c.listenerAddr.IP.String(),
549: 			Port:            c.listenerAddr.Port,
550: 			Expiration:      c.expiration,
551: 			ConnectionLimit: data.GetConnectionLimit(),
552: 			SessionId:       data.GetSessionId(),
553: 		}
554: 
555: 		switch base.Format(c.UI) {
556: 		case "table":
557: 			c.UI.Output(generateSessionInfoTableOutput(sessInfo))
558: 		case "json":
559: 			out, err := json.Marshal(&sessInfo)
560: 			if err != nil {
561: 				c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("error marshaling session information: %w", err).Error())
562: 				return 1
563: 			}
564: 			c.UI.Output(string(out))
565: 		}
566: 	}
567: 
568: 	c.connWg = new(sync.WaitGroup)
569: 
570: 	c.connWg.Add(1)
571: 	go func() {
572: 		defer c.connWg.Done()
573: 		for {
574: 			listeningConn, err := c.listener.AcceptTCP()

577: 				case <-c.proxyCtx.Done():
578: 					return
579: 				case <-c.Context.Done():
580: 					return

583: 					// isn't actually an error condition

615: 				return
616: 			case <-c.Context.Done():

676: func (c *Command) handleConnection(
677: 	listeningConn *net.TCPConn,
678: 	workerAddr string,
679: 	tofuToken string,
680: 	transport *http.Transport) error {
681: 
682: 	defer c.connWg.Done()
683: 
684: 	conn, resp, err := websocket.Dial(
685: 		c.proxyCtx,
686: 		fmt.Sprintf("wss://%s/v1/proxy", workerAddr),
687: 		&websocket.DialOptions{
688: 			HTTPClient: &http.Client{
689: 				Transport: transport,
690: 			},
691: 			Subprotocols: []string{globals.TcpProxyV1},
692: 		},
693: 	)
694: 	if err != nil {
695: 		switch {
696: 		case strings.Contains(err.Error(), "tls: internal error"):
697: 			return errors.New("Session is unauthorized")
698: 		case strings.Contains(err.Error(), "connect: connection refused"):
699: 			return fmt.Errorf("Unable to connect to worker at %s", workerAddr)
700: 		default:
701: 			return fmt.Errorf("Error dialing the worker: %w", err)
702: 		}
703: 	}
704: 
705: 	if resp == nil {
706: 		return errors.New("Response from worker is nil")
707: 	}
708: 	if resp.Header == nil {
709: 		return errors.New("Response header is nil")
710: 	}
711: 	negProto := resp.Header.Get("Sec-WebSocket-Protocol")
712: 	if negProto != globals.TcpProxyV1 {
713: 		return fmt.Errorf("Unexpected negotiated protocol: %s", negProto)
714: 	}
715: 
716: 	handshake := proxy.ClientHandshake{TofuToken: tofuToken}
717: 	if err := wspb.Write(c.proxyCtx, conn, &handshake); err != nil {
718: 		return fmt.Errorf("error sending handshake to worker: %w", err)
719: 	}
720: 	var handshakeResult proxy.HandshakeResult
721: 	if err := wspb.Read(c.proxyCtx, conn, &handshakeResult); err != nil {
722: 		switch {
723: 		case strings.Contains(err.Error(), "unable to authorize connection"):
724: 			// There's no reason to think we'd be able to authorize any more
725: 			// connections after the first has failed
726: 			c.connsLeftCh <- 0
727: 			return errors.New("Unable to authorize connection")
728: 		}
729: 		return fmt.Errorf("error reading handshake result: %w", err)
730: 	}
731: 
732: 	if handshakeResult.GetConnectionsLeft() != -1 {
733: 		c.connsLeftCh <- handshakeResult.GetConnectionsLeft()
734: 	}
735: 
736: 	// Get a wrapped net.Conn so we can use io.Copy
737: 	netConn := websocket.NetConn(c.proxyCtx, conn, websocket.MessageBinary)
738: 
739: 	localWg := new(sync.WaitGroup)
740: 	localWg.Add(2)
741: 
742: 	go func() {
743: 		defer localWg.Done()
744: 		io.Copy(netConn, listeningConn)
745: 		netConn.Close()
746: 		listeningConn.Close()
747: 	}()
748: 	go func() {
749: 		defer localWg.Done()
750: 		io.Copy(listeningConn, netConn)
751: 		listeningConn.Close()
752: 		netConn.Close()
753: 	}()
754: 	localWg.Wait()
755: 
756: 	return nil
757: }
758: 
759: func (c *Command) updateConnsLeft(connsLeft int32) {
760: 	c.connectionsLeft.Store(connsLeft)
761: 
762: 	connInfo := ConnectionInfo{
763: 		ConnectionsLeft: connsLeft,
764: 	}
765: 
766: 	if c.flagExec == "" {
767: 		switch base.Format(c.UI) {
768: 		case "table":
769: 			c.UI.Output(generateConnectionInfoTableOutput(connInfo))
770: 		case "json":
771: 			out, err := json.Marshal(&connInfo)
772: 			if err != nil {
773: 				c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("error marshaling connection information: %w", err).Error())
774: 			}
775: 			c.UI.Output(string(out))
776: 		}
777: 	}
778: }
779: 
780: func (c *Command) handleExec(passthroughArgs []string) {
781: 	defer c.connWg.Done()
782: 	defer c.proxyCancel()
783: 
784: 	port := strconv.Itoa(c.listenerAddr.Port)
785: 	ip := c.listenerAddr.IP.String()
786: 	addr := c.listenerAddr.String()
787: 
788: 	var args []string
789: 
790: 	switch c.Func {
791: 	case "http":
792: 		switch c.flagHttpStyle {
793: 		case "curl":
794: 			if c.flagHttpMethod != "" {
795: 				args = append(args, "-X", c.flagHttpMethod)
796: 			}
797: 			var uri string
798: 			if c.flagHttpHost != "" {
799: 				c.flagHttpHost = strings.TrimSuffix(c.flagHttpHost, "/")
800: 				args = append(args, "-H", fmt.Sprintf("Host: %s", c.flagHttpHost))
801: 				args = append(args, "--resolve", fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s:%s", c.flagHttpHost, port, ip))
802: 				uri = fmt.Sprintf("%s://%s:%s", c.flagHttpScheme, c.flagHttpHost, port)
803: 			} else {
804: 				uri = fmt.Sprintf("%s://%s", c.flagHttpScheme, addr)
805: 			}
806: 			if c.flagHttpPath != "" {
807: 				uri = fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s", uri, strings.TrimPrefix(c.flagHttpPath, "/"))
808: 			}
809: 			args = append(args, uri)
810: 		}
811: 
812: 	case "ssh":
813: 		switch c.flagSshStyle {
814: 		case "ssh":
815: 			args = append(args, "-p", port, ip)
816: 			args = append(args, "-o", fmt.Sprintf("HostKeyAlias=%s", c.sessionAuthz.HostId))
817: 		case "putty":
818: 			args = append(args, "-P", port, ip)
819: 		}
820: 		if c.flagUsername != "" {
821: 			args = append(args, "-l", c.flagUsername)
822: 		}
823: 
824: 	case "postgres":
825: 		switch c.flagPostgresStyle {
826: 		case "psql":
827: 			args = append(args, "-p", port, "-h", ip)
828: 			if c.flagUsername != "" {
829: 				args = append(args, "-U", c.flagUsername)
830: 			}
831: 		}
832: 
833: 	case "rdp":
834: 		switch c.flagRdpStyle {
835: 		case "mstsc":
836: 			args = append(args, "/v", addr)
837: 		}
838: 	}
839: 
840: 	args = append(passthroughArgs, args...)
841: 
842: 	// Might want -t for ssh or -tt but seems fine without it for now...
843: 
844: 	stringReplacer := func(in, typ, replacer string) string {
845: 		for _, style := range []string{
846: 			fmt.Sprintf("{{boundary.%s}}", typ),
847: 			fmt.Sprintf("{{ boundary.%s}}", typ),
848: 			fmt.Sprintf("{{boundary.%s }}", typ),
849: 			fmt.Sprintf("{{ boundary.%s }}", typ),
850: 		} {
851: 			in = strings.Replace(in, style, replacer, -1)
852: 		}
853: 		return in
854: 	}
855: 
856: 	for i := range args {
857: 		args[i] = stringReplacer(args[i], "port", port)
858: 		args[i] = stringReplacer(args[i], "ip", ip)
859: 		args[i] = stringReplacer(args[i], "addr", addr)
860: 	}
861: 
862: 	// NOTE: exec.CommandContext is a hard kill, so if used it leaves the
863: 	// terminal in a weird state. It suffices to simply close the connection,
864: 	// which already happens, so we don't need/want CommandContext here.
865: 	cmd := exec.Command(c.flagExec, args...)
866: 	cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(),
867: 		fmt.Sprintf("BOUNDARY_PROXIED_PORT=%s", port),
868: 		fmt.Sprintf("BOUNDARY_PROXIED_IP=%s", ip),
869: 		fmt.Sprintf("BOUNDARY_PROXIED_ADDR=%s", addr),
870: 	)
871: 	cmd.Stdin = os.Stdin
872: 	cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
873: 	cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
874: 
875: 	if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
876: 		exitCode := 2
877: 
878: 		if exitError, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
879: 			if exitError.Success() {
880: 				c.execCmdReturnValue.Store(0)
881: 				return
882: 			}
883: 			if ws, ok := exitError.Sys().(syscall.WaitStatus); ok {
884: 				c.execCmdReturnValue.Store(int32(ws.ExitStatus()))
885: 				return
886: 			}
887: 		}
888: 
889: 		c.UI.Error(fmt.Sprintf("Failed to run command: %s", err))
890: 		c.execCmdReturnValue.Store(int32(exitCode))
891: 		return
892: 	}
893: 	c.execCmdReturnValue.Store(0)
894: }

229: func (s Service) AuthorizeSession(ctx context.Context, req *pbs.AuthorizeSessionRequest) (*pbs.AuthorizeSessionResponse, error) {
230: 	if err := validateAuthorizeRequest(req); err != nil {
231: 		return nil, err
232: 	}
233: 	authResults := s.authResult(ctx, req.GetId(), action.Authorize)
234: 	if authResults.Error != nil {
235: 		return nil, authResults.Error
236: 	}
237: 	// This could happen if, say, u_recovery was used or u_anon was granted. But
238: 	// don't allow it. It's one thing if grants give access to resources within
239: 	// Boundary, even if those could eventually be used to provide an unintended
240: 	// user access to a remote system. It's quite another to enable anonymous
241: 	// access directly to a remote system.
242: 	//
243: 	// Note that even if u_anon or u_auth are given grants we can still validate
244: 	// a token! So this is just checking that a valid token was provided. The
245: 	// actual reality of this works out to excluding:
246: 	//
247: 	// * True anonymous access (no token provided and u_anon)
248: 	//
249: 	// * u_recovery access (which is fine, recovery is meant for recovering
250: 	// system state, no real reason to allow it to then connect to systems)
251: 	if authResults.AuthTokenId == "" {
252: 		return nil, handlers.ForbiddenError()
253: 	}
254: 
255: 	// Get the target information
256: 	repo, err := s.repoFn()
257: 	if err != nil {
258: 		return nil, err
259: 	}
260: 	t, hostSets, err := repo.LookupTarget(ctx, req.GetId())
261: 	if err != nil {
262: 		if errors.Is(err, db.ErrRecordNotFound) {
263: 			return nil, handlers.NotFoundErrorf("Target %q not found.", req.GetId())
264: 		}
265: 		return nil, err
266: 	}
267: 	if t == nil {
268: 		return nil, handlers.NotFoundErrorf("Target %q not found.", req.GetId())
269: 	}
270: 
271: 	// Instantiate some repos
272: 	sessionRepo, err := s.sessionRepoFn()
273: 	if err != nil {
274: 		return nil, err
275: 	}
276: 	serversRepo, err := s.serversRepoFn()
277: 	if err != nil {
278: 		return nil, err
279: 	}
280: 
281: 	// First, fetch all available hosts. Unless one was chosen in the request,
282: 	// we will pick one at random.
283: 	type compoundHost struct {
284: 		hostSetId string
285: 		hostId    string
286: 	}
287: 
288: 	var chosenId *compoundHost
289: 	requestedId := req.GetHostId()
290: 	staticHostRepo, err := s.staticHostRepoFn()
291: 	if err != nil {
292: 		return nil, err
293: 	}
294: 
295: 	hostIds := make([]compoundHost, 0, len(hostSets)*10)
296: 
297: HostSetIterationLoop:
298: 	for _, tSet := range hostSets {
299: 		hsId := tSet.PublicId
300: 		switch host.SubtypeFromId(hsId) {
301: 		case host.StaticSubtype:
302: 			_, hosts, err := staticHostRepo.LookupSet(ctx, hsId)
303: 			if err != nil {
304: 				return nil, err
305: 			}
306: 			for _, host := range hosts {
307: 				compoundId := compoundHost{hostSetId: hsId, hostId: host.PublicId}
308: 				hostIds = append(hostIds, compoundId)
309: 				if host.PublicId == requestedId {
310: 					chosenId = &compoundId
311: 					break HostSetIterationLoop
312: 				}
313: 			}
314: 		}
315: 	}
316: 	if requestedId != "" && chosenId == nil {
317: 		// We didn't find it
318: 		return nil, handlers.InvalidArgumentErrorf(
319: 			"Errors in provided fields.",
320: 			map[string]string{
321: 				"host_id": "The requested host id is not available.",
322: 			})
323: 	}
324: 	if chosenId == nil {
325: 		if len(hostIds) == 0 {
326: 			// No hosts were found, error
327: 			return nil, handlers.NotFoundErrorf("No hosts found from available target host sets.")
328: 		}
329: 		chosenId = &hostIds[rand.Intn(len(hostIds))]
330: 	}
331: 
332: 	// Generate the endpoint URL
333: 	endpointUrl := &url.URL{
334: 		Scheme: t.GetType(),
335: 	}
336: 	defaultPort := t.GetDefaultPort()
337: 	var endpointHost string
338: 	switch host.SubtypeFromId(chosenId.hostId) {
339: 	case host.StaticSubtype:
340: 		h, err := staticHostRepo.LookupHost(ctx, chosenId.hostId)
341: 		if err != nil {
342: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("error looking up host: %w", err)
343: 		}
344: 		endpointHost = h.Address
345: 		if endpointHost == "" {
346: 			return nil, errors.New("host had empty address")
347: 		}
348: 	}
349: 	if defaultPort != 0 {
350: 		endpointUrl.Host = fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", endpointHost, defaultPort)
351: 	} else {
352: 		endpointUrl.Host = endpointHost
353: 	}
354: 
355: 	expTime := timestamppb.Now()
356: 	expTime.Seconds += int64(t.GetSessionMaxSeconds())
357: 	sessionComposition := session.ComposedOf{
358: 		UserId:          authResults.UserId,
359: 		HostId:          chosenId.hostId,
360: 		TargetId:        t.GetPublicId(),
361: 		HostSetId:       chosenId.hostSetId,
362: 		AuthTokenId:     authResults.AuthTokenId,
363: 		ScopeId:         authResults.Scope.Id,
364: 		Endpoint:        endpointUrl.String(),
365: 		ExpirationTime:  &timestamp.Timestamp{Timestamp: expTime},
366: 		ConnectionLimit: t.GetSessionConnectionLimit(),
367: 	}
368: 
369: 	sess, err := session.New(sessionComposition)
370: 	if err != nil {
371: 		return nil, err
372: 	}
373: 	wrapper, err := s.kmsCache.GetWrapper(ctx, authResults.Scope.Id, kms.KeyPurposeSessions)
374: 	if err != nil {
375: 		return nil, err
376: 	}
377: 	sess, privKey, err := sessionRepo.CreateSession(ctx, wrapper, sess)
378: 	if err != nil {
379: 		return nil, err
380: 	}
381: 
382: 	var workers []*pb.WorkerInfo
383: 	servers, err := serversRepo.ListServers(ctx, servers.ServerTypeWorker)
384: 	if err != nil {
385: 		return nil, err
386: 	}
387: 	for _, v := range servers {
388: 		workers = append(workers, &pb.WorkerInfo{Address: v.Address})
389: 	}
390: 
391: 	sad := &pb.SessionAuthorizationData{
392: 		SessionId:       sess.PublicId,
393: 		TargetId:        t.GetPublicId(),
394: 		Scope:           authResults.Scope,
395: 		CreatedTime:     sess.CreateTime.GetTimestamp(),
396: 		Type:            t.GetType(),
397: 		Certificate:     sess.Certificate,
398: 		PrivateKey:      privKey,
399: 		WorkerInfo:      workers,
400: 		ConnectionLimit: t.GetSessionConnectionLimit(),
401: 	}
402: 	marshaledSad, err := proto.Marshal(sad)
403: 	if err != nil {
404: 		return nil, err
405: 	}
406: 	encodedMarshaledSad := base58.FastBase58Encoding(marshaledSad)
407: 
408: 	ret := &pb.SessionAuthorization{
409: 		SessionId:          sess.PublicId,
410: 		TargetId:           t.GetPublicId(),
411: 		Scope:              authResults.Scope,
412: 		CreatedTime:        sess.CreateTime.GetTimestamp(),
413: 		Type:               t.GetType(),
414: 		AuthorizationToken: string(encodedMarshaledSad),
415: 		UserId:             authResults.UserId,
416: 		HostId:             chosenId.hostId,
417: 		HostSetId:          chosenId.hostSetId,
418: 	}
419: 	return &pbs.AuthorizeSessionResponse{Item: ret}, nil
420: }

613: func (s Service) authResult(ctx context.Context, id string, a action.Type) auth.VerifyResults {
614: 	res := auth.VerifyResults{}
615: 
616: 	var parentId string
617: 	opts := []auth.Option{auth.WithType(resource.Target), auth.WithAction(a)}
618: 	switch a {
619: 	case action.List, action.Create:
620: 		parentId = id
621: 		iamRepo, err := s.iamRepoFn()
622: 		if err != nil {
623: 			res.Error = err
624: 			return res
625: 		}
626: 		scp, err := iamRepo.LookupScope(ctx, parentId)
627: 		if err != nil {
628: 			res.Error = err
629: 			return res
630: 		}
631: 		if scp == nil {
632: 			res.Error = handlers.NotFoundError()
633: 			return res
634: 		}
635: 	default:
636: 		repo, err := s.repoFn()
637: 		if err != nil {
638: 			res.Error = err
639: 			return res
640: 		}
641: 		t, _, err := repo.LookupTarget(ctx, id)
642: 		if err != nil {
643: 			res.Error = err
644: 			return res
645: 		}
646: 		if t == nil {
647: 			res.Error = handlers.NotFoundError()
648: 			return res
649: 		}
650: 		parentId = t.GetScopeId()
651: 		opts = append(opts, auth.WithId(id))
652: 	}
653: 	opts = append(opts, auth.WithScopeId(parentId))
654: 	return auth.Verify(ctx, opts...)
655: }

657: func toProto(in target.Target, m []*target.TargetSet) (*pb.Target, error) {
658: 	out := pb.Target{
659: 		Id:                     in.GetPublicId(),
660: 		ScopeId:                in.GetScopeId(),
661: 		CreatedTime:            in.GetCreateTime().GetTimestamp(),
662: 		UpdatedTime:            in.GetUpdateTime().GetTimestamp(),
663: 		Version:                in.GetVersion(),
664: 		Type:                   target.TcpTargetType.String(),
665: 		SessionMaxSeconds:      wrapperspb.UInt32(in.GetSessionMaxSeconds()),
666: 		SessionConnectionLimit: wrapperspb.Int32(in.GetSessionConnectionLimit()),
667: 	}
668: 	if in.GetDescription() != "" {
669: 		out.Description = wrapperspb.String(in.GetDescription())
670: 	}
671: 	if in.GetName() != "" {
672: 		out.Name = wrapperspb.String(in.GetName())
673: 	}
674: 	attrs := &pb.TcpTargetAttributes{}
675: 	if in.GetDefaultPort() > 0 {
676: 		attrs.DefaultPort = &wrappers.UInt32Value{Value: in.GetDefaultPort()}
677: 	}
678: 	st, err := handlers.ProtoToStruct(attrs)
679: 	if err != nil {
680: 		return nil, handlers.ApiErrorWithCodeAndMessage(codes.Internal, "failed building password attribute struct: %v", err)
681: 	}
682: 	out.Attributes = st
683: 	for _, hs := range m {
684: 		out.HostSetIds = append(out.HostSetIds, hs.GetPublicId())
685: 		out.HostSets = append(out.HostSets, &pb.HostSet{
686: 			Id:            hs.GetPublicId(),
687: 			HostCatalogId: hs.GetCatalogId(),
688: 		})
689: 	}
690: 	return &out, nil
691: }

702: func validateCreateRequest(req *pbs.CreateTargetRequest) error {
703: 	return handlers.ValidateCreateRequest(req.GetItem(), func() map[string]string {
704: 		badFields := map[string]string{}
705: 		if !handlers.ValidId(scope.Project.Prefix(), req.GetItem().GetScopeId()) {
706: 			badFields["scope_id"] = "This field is required to have a properly formatted project scope id."
707: 		}
708: 		if req.GetItem().GetName() == nil || req.GetItem().GetName().GetValue() == "" {
709: 			badFields["name"] = "This field is required."
710: 		}
711: 		if req.GetItem().GetSessionConnectionLimit() != nil {
712: 			val := req.GetItem().GetSessionConnectionLimit().GetValue()
713: 			switch {
714: 			case val == -1:
715: 			case val > 0:
716: 			default:
717: 				badFields["session_connection_limit"] = "This must be -1 (unlimited) or greater than zero."
718: 			}
719: 		}
